Short CV

Assoc.Prof. Belkıs Atasever Arslan was born in 1980 in Istanbul. In 2001, he graduated from Istanbul University, Department of Biology. She received his master's degree in 2004 and doctorate degree in 2008 in the Department of Physiology at Istanbul University, Istanbul Medical Faculty.

In 2009, she was one of three researchers winning researcher postdoctoral fellowship in Turkish Academy of Sciences. In the same year, she also received a postdoctoral scholarship from TUBITAK and had worked as post doc in Sabancı University, Department of Biological Sciences and Bioengineering for 3 years.

She started to work as an assistant professor at Üsküdar University in 2012. In 2018, she received the title of Associate Professor in the field of Medical Physiology. In 2019, she was appointed as Adjunct Associate Professor to the Department of Anatomy and Cell Biology, School of Medicine, University of Illinois at Chicago. Assoc.Prof. Belkıs Atasever Arslan has 2 European patent registrations and several patent applications. She works on drug discovery, immunology and signal transduction.

She is married and has two children.
